name: Aloha Pickleball
description:
Interested in joining us for all the fun?
 
Welcome to Aloha Pickleball, your premier indoor pickleball destination!

We’re excited to welcome all our new members to an action-packed year filled with events, activities, and plenty of pickleball fun. Thank you for choosing us as your go-to spot for the game you love.

Court Rental Rate:
$50 per hour
Available for private party’s 

How to Reserve an Open Court:

Click Book a Court at the top of the page.
Select a time section to view availability.
Navigate to your preferred date using the arrows or by clicking the date at the top-left corner.
Click the Reserve button for your desired time slot.
Fill out the reservation form, including the number of spots needed for you and your family.
You’ll receive an email confirmation once your reservation is complete.

Need Assistance?
Our friendly staff is here to help! Feel free to reach out if you have questions, need to reserve a court, or want to cancel a reservation. Jackie (808) 769-2922

Let’s make this year unforgettable—on and off the court!
fees:
Private — $50.00 Per Hour up to 9 players allowed 
Private Party Bookings Available 
No Yearly Membership Fees 
No Lines 
Professional Court

name: Hidden Valley Lake
description:
The courts are accessible and restricted to Hidden Valley Lake Association (HVLA) members and residents of the community. 

If you know someone who lives in community and can get a key, they can let you into the courts as guests. There isn’t any active security or monitoring of the courts. 

The courts have permanent pickleball lines and permanent tennis nets. The net height can be adjusted with cranks and center straps. There is a wooden post in the facility that has markings for pickleball and tennis heights for reference.
address: 18174 Hidden Valley Rd. Hidden Valley Lake
open_play_schedule: HVL Pickleball Group meets on Tuesday and Thursday evenings from 5pm to 8:30pm. Courts are open during this time.
fees: Members only — Keys available to residents at the HVLA office for $7.

name: Lang Park
description:
California town now requiring quiet paddles at public pickleball courts
Apr 10 2025
Pickleball players who play at Lang Park in Laguna Beach, California, will be required to use "quiet" paddles going forward after the city council there passed a law that fines people for using the louder traditional paddles.

The Police Department’s Park Rangers will enforce the new law, city officials said.

The city council took action after several years of complaints from residents of a nearby senior community, according to The Orange County Register.

In 2023, the city installed sound barriers around the courts, which were tennis courts originally and were converted to pickleball as the sport gained popularity. That apparently didn't solve the problem, as complaints continued, resulting in the city also limiting hours at the park -- no play is allowed on Mondays or in the afternoon on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

name: Memorial Park - Santa Monica
description:
There are two entities that operate at Memorial Park: the Santa Monica Pickleball Club (SMPC), a non-profit club; and the City of Santa Monica. 

There is pickleball play every day, but the only set schedules are these:

The Club’s Open Play (free): 
M-F, 8 AM-12 PM 

The City’s Drop-In Program (pay-to-play):
M/W night: 5 PM-9:30 PM
Sat/Sun: 8 AM-11 AM; 11:15 AM-2 PM
open_play_schedule: See City of Santa Monica website for latest. Drop-In Pickleball is M and W, 5 - 9:30pm; S and Su, 8am to 2pm.

name: Pickleball Badminton Center
description:
Our recently inaugurated pickleball facility, located in the delightful city of Lakewood near Long Beach Airport, is approaching its two-month anniversary. We take pride in offering five indoor courts equipped with joint-friendly cushioning to minimize the risk of injuries. Please don't hesitate to contact us for additional information. You can find us at 3699 Industry Ave, Lakewood, California.

Walk-ins are welcome, and the fee is $10 for individuals aged 64 and below, $5 for seniors aged 65 and above, and complimentary for active police and fire personnel. Indoor court shoes are mandatory, and if you don't have your own, we provide shoe rentals at $5 per pair. For those seeking exclusive court time, you can gather a group of up to 8 players for a rate of $30 per hour per court.
Come rain or shine, we eagerly anticipate welcoming all pickleball enthusiasts to our venue!

name: Pickleball Paradise
description:
Play is by court reservations only at $35 per hour.

2 Membership passes are available. The Pickle Pass at $99 per month includes: 5-1-hour court reservations for you and a guest, use of indoor clubhouse, Gatorade and snacks.

The Paradise Pass - $148 per month includes: 2/2hr court reservations and 2/1 hour court reservations plus use of the entire facility up to 2 hours for up to 3 guests, to include:  12 seat swim spa/Jacuzzi, private cabanas, frisbee golf, corn hole, putting green and volleyball.

Both memberships include 20% off of private event rentals, $5 off Round Robin admissions and an invitation for 2 to our annual 4th of July customer appreciation gala that features a live band, cocktails, dinner buffet and a raffle to win a variety of different prizes.  Not to mention the city of Lake Elsinore's amazing fireworks display from the lake!

name: Portola Springs Community Park
description:
6 dedicated courts (Courts 1 thru 6) and 2 hybrid courts (Courts A&B). Courts A&B are available when not in use by tennis players. Pickleballers maintain priority once playing on Courts A&B.

Courts 1&2 and A&B are for pro teaching classes. All teaching pros are officially authorized by the city of Irvine. Their photos are on the outside of court #2, and their schedules are on the door. They can also have private lessons in those courts whenever they needed. The four courts are only open when no teaching pro is teaching. Please don't challenge the teaching pro for an open court (such as Court 2 or Court A/B) even it is not used at that moment. Teaching pro can have a class up to 8 students, and those students pay for using court #1 & 2. Not just court #1. 

Reservations are possible for courts 5 & 6. Check the city website for reserving a court at www.yourirvine.org. A group is limited to a single reservation per day. 

The courts have adapted an open play by convention, where a player can place a paddle at the side of the net post and claim the next game on that court. That convention may not coincide with the posted rules for the courts by the city of Irvine, but is widely used by consensus during the day.

Groups occupying any of the courts for more than two hours must vacate the courts for those in waiting. Players are friendly and courteous and expect you to be the same. Start by introducing your name, and let the fun begin.

For any issues, such as safety or injury incidents, call the Park's office at (949) 724-6192.
open_play_schedule: Each reservation can be for a min. of 1hr and max. of 2hrs. Reservations are possible between 8:00am-12:00pm & 4:00pm - 10:00pm 7 days/week. Rental facility permit should be presented upon request. Courts are open for drop ins when no reservation or no show.

With the resurfacing of two tennis courts in Central Park near the Community Recreation Center (CRC), the City of Santa Clara has taken this opportunity to cater to the growing interest in pickleball by transforming the two courts into a flexible community recreational area. Six pickleball courts with blended lines have been introduced for a better multi-sport racquet experience for the community.

Players must check in by phone or in person before going to their assigned court.

Reservations can be made by phone at 408-247-0178 or online.

Lifetime Activities will be providing pickleball nets during designated pickleball hours. Once the pickleball hours conclude, tennis play will resume and the courts will remain illuminated until 10 p.m. 

Pro shop is located at 2625 Hayward Drive, Santa Clara, CA 95051

 Best, 

Anthony Harding

name: Sports Court
description:
We are excited to have you and wanted to go over some ground rules.  Please note that this rental contract is for the court's use only. The area around the court is still under gardening construction. We ask that you please respect this area and stay off it, as we have tools and uneven dirt around it.  

Immerse yourself in the excitement of training amidst the awe-inspiring natural surroundings of an outdoor training facility. Our versatile sports court caters to soccer / Futsal, basketball, and pickleball, providing the ideal synergy of athletic training and the serenity of the great outdoors.  

Space size:  Our court is 32x60, about 1,920 sq ft. 
Pickleball:  Bring your pedals and balls.  Net will be provided.  

If you are planning to stay and watch, there is a small viewing area. We have 10 plastic chairs. Please feel free to bring your Bluetooth speakers to play music.

1) Please park on the street.  Do not park in the driveway.  

2) Enter through the left gate and follow the sign.  

3) Please refrain from running on the court to prevent accidents or injuries. 

4) No restroom on site as it is attached to the resident.  
Food and drinks are okay, but there is NO FOOD OR DRINKS on the court.  No coolers with wheels.  You must be able to carry it down.  

5)  No wheelchair or stroller access to the court.  It's all steps.

6)  Please do not leave any trash behind.

The facility includes amenities like food, restrooms, water, and locker rooms. Reservations are not available. A membership is required to play.

name: Yountville Community Center
description: Yountville Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Yountville, CA. There are 6 courts. 4 are outdoors and 2 are indoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Yountville Vineyard Park
description: Yountville Vineyard Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Yountville, CA. There are 4 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Ysmael Villegas Community Center
description: Ysmael Villegas Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Riverside, CA. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms, wheelchair accessibility, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Yucaipa Community Center
description: Yucaipa Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Yucaipa, CA. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: Zerrillo Park
description: The gate to these courts is locked and pad locked. To obtain a key you have to go to Riverbank city offices, fill out a form, and pay a fee.
open_play_schedule: They are only open Mondays through Thursdays.
